reasoning: Martinique is a relatively stable French overseas department. While there have been protests concerning the cost of living, political violence resulting in fatalities is exceedingly rare. Reaching 11 or more ACLED-recorded fatalities (which would be >10x the baseline of 1.0) in a single 30-day period would require an unprecedented catastrophic event or extremely deadly state-civilian friction, far surpassing the intensity of the 2024 New Caledonia riots. Given the historical base rate of near-zero fatalities for this region, the probability of exceeding this threshold is extremely low.
